PET plastic: Remove the cap (dispose separately in plastic bin)
Flatten the bottle to save space
Place in the Yellow Bin (Gelbe Tonne) or plastic collection bag
glass: Remove caps and lids (dispose in metal recycling)
Sort by color: white glass, green glass, brown glass
Bring to the nearest glass container (Glascontainer)
paper: Remove any plastic wrapping or tape
Place in the Blue Bin (Altpapiertonne) or bring to paper container
organic: Compostable items only: food scraps, garden waste, coffee grounds
e-waste: Do NOT place in regular bins
Bring to a municipal collection point (Altstoffsammelzentrum)
Many electronics stores also accept small e-waste for free

Austria.notes: Austria uses a color-coded bin system. Deposit-return (Pfand) applies to some bottles.

PET plastic: Check for Pfand deposit symbol — if present, return to a supermarket Pfandautomat
If no deposit: empty, rinse, and flatten
Place in the Yellow Bin (Gelbe Tonne) or Yellow Bag (Gelber Sack)
glass: Sort by color: Weißglas, Grünglas, Braunglas
Bring to the nearest Glascontainer (not in household bins)
Note: glass containers have restricted hours in residential areas
paper: Remove any plastic windows from envelopes
Place in the Blue Bin (Blaue Tonne)
organic: Place in the Brown Bin (Braune Tonne / Biotonne)
Accepted: fruit/vegetable scraps, coffee filters, eggshells, garden waste
Not accepted: cooked food, meat, dairy (varies by municipality)
e-waste: Do NOT place in regular bins — illegal in Germany
Bring to a municipal recycling center (Wertstoffhof)
Retailers with >400m² sales area must accept small e-waste
Batteries: return to any store that sells batteries (collection boxes at entrance)

Germany.notes: Germany has an extensive Pfand deposit system for bottles and cans. The Duales System manages packaging recycling.

PET plastic: Rinse thoroughly — contaminated items will be rejected
Place in the designated plastic recycling area on collection day
glass: Sort by color if required by your district
Place in the glass recycling area on collection day
paper: Bundle newspapers and magazines with string
Place in the paper recycling area on collection day
organic: IMPORTANT: You must purchase designated food waste bags (종량제 봉투) from convenience stores or supermarkets
Bag prices vary by district and volume (typically ₩100-500 per bag)
Place sealed bags in the food waste bin (음식물 쓰레기통) in your apartment complex or designated area
e-waste: Call your district office (구청) to arrange large e-waste pickup
Small electronics can be dropped at community recycling centers
Some manufacturers offer free pickup for old appliances

South Korea.notes: South Korea requires volume-based waste bags (종량제 봉투) purchased at stores. Recycling must be sorted and put out on specific collection days. Food waste is collected separately and often weighed in apartment complexes.
